title.zh: 一首三身的帝俊后裔之国
title.en: One Head, Three Bodies: Descendants of Di Jun

summary.zh: 大荒之中一首而三身的国度，帝俊之妻娥皇所生，姚姓，以黍为食，能驱使虎豹熊罴四兽；海外西经把它列在夏后启之北。
summary.en: A wilderness nation whose people have one head and three bodies, born of Di Jun's wife Ehuang. Surnamed Yao, they eat millet and command four beasts; another chapter places them north of Qi of the Xia.

description.zh:
大荒南经记：大荒之中有一种人，一个脑袋下长着三副身体。帝俊之妻娥皇生下了这个三身之国，国人姓姚，吃黍子，能驱使「四鸟」——大荒诸经他处开列这四鸟的名单，其实是豹、虎、熊、罴四种猛兽，「鸟」在此是兽的泛称。海外西经也记有此国：「三身国在夏后启之北，一首而三身」，《淮南子·墬形训》把三身民列入海外三十六国的名录。

这一国的谱系埋着线索。姚是舜的姓氏，娥皇又与嫁给舜的尧之长女同名——袁珂《山海经校注》即以此为「帝俊即舜」说的重要证据：大荒经里帝俊娶娥皇生姚姓之国，与传世文献里舜姓姚、妻娥皇，是同一传说的两副面孔。

海内经还给三身国安排了后代：帝俊生三身，三身生义均，义均就是「巧倕」，开始为下界民众发明各种工巧。一首三身的怪形之国，就这样接进了上古发明家的谱系——在《山海经》的世界里，形体的奇异与血统的尊贵、技艺的传承并行不悖。
description.en:
In the midst of the great wilderness, says the southern classic, live people with one head and three bodies. Di Jun's wife Ehuang bore this nation; its people are surnamed Yao, eat millet, and command the 'four birds' — a list that other wilderness passages spell out as leopard, tiger, and the two great bears, 'bird' serving as a loose word for beast. The Beyond the Seas: West chapter locates the country north of Qi, lord of the Xia, and the Huainanzi counts the Three-Bodied among the thirty-six nations beyond the seas.

The genealogy carries a buried clue. Yao is the surname of the sage-king Shun, and Ehuang is the name of the elder daughter of Yao the sage-king, given to Shun in marriage. Yuan Ke's critical commentary treats this as key evidence that Di Jun and Shun are one figure split in two: in the wilderness classics Di Jun marries Ehuang and fathers a Yao-surnamed nation; in the transmitted histories Shun bears the surname Yao and marries Ehuang — the same tradition wearing two faces.

The final chapter grafts the nation into the lineage of invention: Di Jun begat Three-Body, Three-Body begat Yijun, and Yijun — the craftsman Qiao Chui — first devised the hundred crafts for the people below. A nation of prodigious anatomy is thus also an ancestor of technology; in this book, strange bodies and noble arts descend together.

value.en: Born of Di Jun's wife Ehuang; surnamed Yao
Millet-eaters who command leopard, tiger, and bears

sources.zh: 《山海经·大荒南经》《海外西经》《海内经》
sources.en: Classic of Mountains and Seas, 'Great Wilderness: South', 'Beyond the Seas: West', and the final chapter
Yuan Ke, Critical Commentary on the Classic of Mountains and Seas

volume.en: Classic of the Great Wilderness: South
